author: @bradleyconroy4500
text: If you have the means, please save backup copies of videos on this channel.  YouTube has already  (temporarily? ) shut down a similar channel, Cody's Lab.  Don't let Alphabet burn down the new Library of Alexandria with all of its contents.

author: @Bytelogicsystems
text: For those of us who are remote,  would there be any possibility of joining remotely? For even the inn house workshops.

replies.author: @emilyfwg966
replies.text: We will be looking into webinars etc as Patti stated in our video, unfortunately it wouldn't be feasible for the first workshop but we could look to possibly incorporate this next time.

author: @ericlinz4626
text:
Look back on this video:
https://youtu.be/Ue82M9nyS2I
...Cause and Effect is the root of all conflict.
That bad aspect of science is that Cause and Effect is both rigid and wrong.
To restore the true vision of what science was supposed to be you need to recreate that desire to assess Cause and Effect at a level where you comprehend that Cause branches outward as you go backwards in time.  This is difficult for many to comprehend.  The true Cause of an Effect will branch outwards backwards in time.  If you go back far enough the Cause is so diluted as to be not related.  Think about how Cause and Effect really functions...  it's not a rigid sort of thing. (Narrow Gate vs Wide Gate thinking...  the bible has about five references to this concept) Reality doesn't flow in a simple linear way...  every moment is modified by many possible Causes.  We can (through our will) bend reality and if we follow Natural Laws (Hermetic Laws) we can create a future that is different.  We might harness the power of capacitance (battery) on a level far in excess of the Permittivity of Free Space. (this the goal)

author: @Web3Dre
text: I don't understand this. I assume people following this channel are geographically spread around the world. So why would anyone make an irl workshop and not a webinar or an online workshop? I would gladly pay for an online webinar. I can't help but thinking this is an unfortunate strategy.

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: As mentioned, we are also looking at the idea of webinars. This is to allow those who are able to visit to work on their own ideas in the lab with Rob and Steve - and the global spread is why we asked if there would be interest before deciding to try it!

author: @Lastaii
text: What a brilliant idea! Hope it goes well and you can do these on a regular basis :D

replies.author: @pattimurray-smith6905
replies.text: If the first one goes well, we'll look at doing that!
